summary: Get All Time Off for a Single Person
description: Retrieves all Time Off entries for a specified Person within a company.

parameters.name: company_id
parameters.description:
Unique identifier for the company. This parameter accepts both formats:
- **Recommended**: Procore company ID (integer) - Use this for new integrations
- Legacy: LaborChart UUID format (uuid string...

parameters.name: timezone
parameters.description: The timezone in which to order time off entries.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The number of time off records to be returned in a single request. Default is 40.

parameters.name: start_after
parameters.description: Time off ID used for pagination.

schema.description: A collection of time off records for a single person.

data.description: List of time off records associated with the person.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Time Off record.

person_id.description: ID of the person associated with this Time Off entry.

company_id.description: ID of the company this Time Off is associated with.

start_day.description: Start date of the time off (MM/DD/YY).

end_day.description: End date of the time off (MM/DD/YY).

is_paid.description: Whether the time off is paid.

repeat.description: The repeat interval for recurring time off.

repeat_end_day.description: The end date of the repeating time off.

cadence.description: Frequency of repetition (e.g., every 1 week, every 2 weeks).

apply_to_saturday.description: Whether the time off applies to Saturdays.

apply_to_sunday.description: Whether the time off applies to Sundays.

batch_start_time.description: Start time of the time off (formatted as HH:MM am/pm).

batch_end_time.description: End time of the time off (formatted as HH:MM am/pm).

instances.description: List of instances for repeating time off.

pagination.description: Pagination metadata for the time off records.

limit.description: The number of records returned in the response.

next_starting_after.description: The ID of the last record in the response, used for pagination.

previous_starting_before.description: The ID of the first record in the response, used for reverse pagination.

total_possible.description: The total number of records available.
